name: Archway Passage
description: A corridor with a repeating arch motif overhead. Floor of @floor, two lateral walls of @primary along the longer (length) axis, and a sequence of arch ribs at the top — every 3 blocks along the passage a complete arch is built (two @primary jambs supplied by the lateral walls, two inward-facing @stairs at the top corners to fake the curve, a flat @primary top course, and an @accent keystone at the apex). The bays between successive arches are left fully open overhead so daylight (or a higher storey) spills into the passage. A @light sits on every keystone, and 1-2 @carpet runners line the floor. Short ends remain open so the passage acts as a connector.

required_furniture.rule: Solid @floor plane at y0 spanning the full footprint of the passage.
Two @primary lateral walls along the longer axis, full height (y0+1 .. y1); short ends remain open so the passage is a true connector.

Arch ribs every 3 blocks along the passage length: top course of @primary jamb-to-jamb plus (when interior width >= 2) two inward-facing @stairs at the top corners. At least 3 arches whenever the corridor is >= 6 blocks long; the very narrowest passages (interior width 1) skip the corner stairs because the apex column coincides with the inner column — only the keystone remains.
One @accent keystone at the apex centre of each arch rib (same count as the arches).
A @light block sits on (or directly under) every keystone so each bay is pooled with light from above.
One centred @carpet runner for narrow passages (interior width <= 2); two parallel edge-aligned runners for wider passages.

placement_rules: Open span between arches admits light
Walls run along the LONGER horizontal axis; the shorter axis is the corridor width
Short ends are open (no end walls) so the passage connects to adjacent rooms or to the outside
Inward-facing @stairs at the top corners of every rib visually round the opening into an arch
Defensive on AABBs from 3x4x6 up to 5x5x16; oversize inputs clamp to the supported envelope and undersize inputs return no ops
lighting_ventilation: Each keystone carries a @light (torch on medieval, redstone lamp on modern, sea lantern on fantasy) so the passage is rhythmically pooled with light under every arch. Between arches the overhead span is OPEN — admitting daylight and natural airflow from above, exactly as Alexander recommends in *Pools of Light*. Combined with the open short ends, the passage ventilates and illuminates itself without needing windows in the lateral walls.

medieval.notes: Cobblestone jambs and arch ribs with stone-brick keystones, a stone-brick floor, cobblestone-stair corbels and torch crowns — reads as the cloister of a castle bailey.

modern.notes: Quartz arch ribs over an andesite floor with smooth-stone keystones and redstone-lamp uplights — reads as a minimalist civic colonnade.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak ribs with purpur keystones and sea-lantern lights over a mossy-cobblestone floor — reads as an enchanted forest cloister or a wizard-tower passage.
